hits.title: Looking for someone to survey/certify a level surface for a Guinness ...
hits.summary: Guinness World Records mandates that I have a licensed land surveyor to accurately measure both the distance and the slope of the terrain.

hits.title: How to collect and submit evidence | Guinness World Records
hits.summary:
Before you attempt a Guinness World Records title, you need to carefully read your record guidelines.
These guidelines lay out the rules for your attempt as well as detailing all the evidence you need to provide us with afterwards.
...
This document contains all the important information needed to prepare you on the road to gathering and submitting your evidence.
It includes information regarding witnesses, timekeepers, stewards and the use of log books.
It also has guidance for producing better video and photographic evidence.
...
A cover letter is required for **ALL records.** This is your opportunity to provide Guinness World Records with a clear overview of your record attempt.
It’s a final summary before submitting your evidence, as it will provide a road map for our records management team when evaluating the accompanying evidence.
...
Every record attempt requires **a minimum of two witnesses**.
Witnesses must complete this statement.
...
Records involving timeframes or based on speed will require timekeepers to be present.
Timekeepers must complete these statements
...
For mass participation records we require stewards to supervise groups of up to 50 participants.
Stewards must complete these statements.
## Endurance marathon log book template
This template is for “longest marathon” records only.
Any other records that require logbooks will require you to create your own.

hits.title: [PDF] Guide To Your Evidence | Guinness World Records
What evidence does Guinness World Records require? - Facebook
[PDF] RECORD BREAKERS PACK GENERAL EVIDENCE ...
hits.summary:
GENERAL EVIDENCE 
REQUIREMENTS 
For the purposes of verifying any record attempt, the 
following material must be provided:  
1. Cover letter 
2. Two witness statements or signed affidavit by a public 
notary 
3. Independent corroboration in the form of media coverage
4. Video footage of the record attempt on VHS, DVD or CD 
ROM video 
5. High quality colour photographs 
6. Guideline-specific evidence: log books, official statements 
and measurements 
7. Schedule One  - Guidance On Supporting  Materials & 
Evidence 
8. Schedule Two - Record Claimant’s Evidence Submission
…
5) The statements should confirm that the guidelines have been followed and 
state the exact details pertaining to how the record is quantified - dimensions, 
participants involved, etc.
along with the date, location, record title and holder.
6) For record attempts that take place over several hours or days, it is possible
for more than two witnesses to be present in ‘shifts’.
However, all the 
witnesses must meet the criteria set out above and must submit statements 
as described above in chronological order, where possible.
Each statement 
must make clear exactly when that particular witness was ‘on duty’.
There 
should always be at least two independent witnesses present at any time.
7) If providing notarised statements, they should follow the details outlined 
above in terms of content.
...
You will need to submit photographs as part of your world record claim.
Photographs 
may submitted in digital format on CD-ROM or DVD (taken at 300dpi in a 15cm x 
15cm size), or in traditional photographic print or slide format.
Note that colour
...
Slides, traditional print (negative) photographs, as well as digital images are suitable.
Digital images must be at least 15cm x 15 cm in size and taken at 300dpi.
Colour 
photocopies and prints of digital images are not acceptable unless accompanied by 
digital versions of the images on CD ROM or DVD.
...
GUIDELINE SPECIFIC EVIDENCE 
With many of our record categories, specific evidence to show us continual 
monitoring and high standards of measurement must be given.
Here is a brief list of 
some of these, however, you should consult the guidelines document in order to find 
out the specific additional material you need to send in.
Examples of these are:
...
• 
Expert statements 
o These can be from many sources – e.g. environmental health officer 
or public health official for food records, vets for animal records, 
sporting officials, surveyors for distances and sizes 
o Accurate professional equipment should be used for measuring and 
all relevant measures must be given.
o Measurements should ideally be given in metric (meters, grams, 
kilograms), if not then in imperial (feet, pounds, ounces) or preferably 
both.
10. 
Schedule One , Two and Three 
This must be signed and returned to GWR after you have made your attempt.
Do 
NOT send it before you have done so.
This allows you and anyone else who is
…
• 
Guinness World Records 
3rd Floor 
184-192 Drummond Street 
London 
NW1 3HP 
UNITED KINGDOM 
• 
Clearly mark all documentation and photographs with your name and address, 
Reference/Claim ID and stating which record has been attempted.
• 
All material may be retained for possible archiving.

hits.title: Record Rules - World UltraCycling Association
hits.summary:
- fixed-distance records English (100 mile, 200 mile, 300 mile, 500 mile and 1000 mile) or metric (100km, 200km, 300km, 500km, and 1000km) on surveyed road courses or track (indoor or outdoor)- for both World and Country (Rider Nationality) categories
...
The rider must ride at least 90% of the documented time of the listed time of the record and complete a reasonable amount of distance for their combined categories when compared to other existing records with similar categories.
...
The rider must complete the distance in a reasonable amount of time for their combined categories when compared to other existing records with similar categories.
This is to prevent someone from riding the distance at a very slow speed just to claim the 1st record in that category.
...
For extremely long rides that may require a boat, train, or plane to cross a span, or island certain courses may impose a minimum amount of miles: Those courses are listed here:
- USA W-E or E-W (oldest) – 3200 miles
- Europe N-S or S-N – 6000 Km
- Japan W-E or E-W – 2400 Km
Note: New courses and minimum distances may be created by the Records Committee and added to the above if needed.
The Record Chair’s decision regarding if a record meets these minimum requirements to be certified is final.
**NOTE #1**: Rider age is determined by the actual age at the end date of the record attempt (This is the date that they actual record is “set”.
Attempts by riders under 18 will require permission of the Records Chairman and waiver signed by a parent or guardian.
Teams will use the average age of team members, except that for 8-person teams there are no age or gender categories.
...
The post-ride report shall include route information which may be any or all of the following: link to an online GPS map of the attempt, physical map with the route highlighted, or turn-by-turn log of the route.
...
Prior to a record attempt, the WUCA official will inspect all vehicles (for proper lighting, signage, license plates, registration and insurance coverage) and bicycles (for proper lighting and reflective tape).
In addition, each crew member who will be driving any vehicle will be required to show a driver’s license valid for the jurisdiction(s) of the record attempt.
Failure to comply with this rule may result in immediate disqualification.
A record of the inspections will be submitted with the Official’s Report.
...
If due to traffic, private MUP trails that do not allow motorized vehicles, or local authorities require a leap-frog strategy on public roads that may require the WUCA official to be out of a “line-of-site” view of the rider for a significant amount of time or multiple times during the attempt, the rider is required: #1- to collect on-board GPS track, Power, and HR data for the entire period of the day(s) that line-of-sight view is not possible, but also including times when it is.
For example, if there are 8 hours where the rider will be on and off MUPs, then the supplementary data collection should occur the entire 8 hours.
This data must be submitted with the other evidence as post-attempt documentation via a public Strava link, and #2 – the official must note on the log what times they were not able to visually view the rider.
...
If the record attempt will last more than 24 hours, at least two WUCA officials are required, except that an attempt may proceed with one official if the official is given at least 8 consecutive hours off duty during each 24 hours.
In this situation, the rider cannot proceed while the official is off duty.
...
For road attempts, the WUCA official must record rider progress every 10-15 miles, or every 45-60 minutes, whichever comes first.
The Log Sheet should indicate time, mileage, and sufficient road or landmark detail to determine the location on a map.
The WUCA official(s) must write and sign the WUCA Official’s Summary, the Record Attempt Summary form and Log Sheets before they are submitted as part of the Record Attempt Report to the WUCA Records Chair following the attempt.
…
- collect GPS data with an onboard head unit for the entire ride.
(GPS & temp needed for all attempts. HR an Power required for any Guinness submission)
- take a minimum of 3 GPS tagged photos each day showing the rider in the frame with at least one showing the rider’s face): one at the start, one during the ride and one at the end.
- Note, the official may also take these GPS photos as long as they are not driving the follow vehicle at the time.
- If this method fails, then the official must log manually into a logbook at the start, every 4 hours, and at the end while not driving the follow vehicle.
- take a minimum of one (1) 10 sec GPS tagged video at some point during the day.
- note, the official may also take this GPS video as long as they are not driving the follow vehicle at the time.
- If this method fails, then the official or rider must secure a witness signature at some location on the course for that day.
- Upload the gxp file, the photos, and the video to Strava
- Download the fit from the GPS unit and send within 24-hrs after the entire attempt is complete.
- Use of a tracker is not required, but highly encouraged – Garmin Live-Track works well.
...
Times for all point-to-point records are rounded to the nearest minute.
The WUCA official riding in the follow vehicle is to record the start and end time as well as intermediate times on the log sheet as the attempt is progressing.
Times for all fixed-distance or fixed-time attempts are recorded to the nearest second.
...
Penalties for fixed-time (12 Hour and 24 Hour) records shall be distance penalties and shall be cumulative, per the following schedule:
...
If a section of a road is determined by your official to be too dangerous to ride, your official must do the following: take pics of the road that is determined unsafe, drive you past the unsafe section while taking a GPS track of the section you are being driven across.
Once you are in a safe location to ride again, then stop and save the GPS so it can be saved and sent in as part of the evidence.
Then at that point you are continuing on your course: ride exactly 50% of the distance you road in the car and then return back to that spot.
Now you have made up the distance.
The official should note the time and distance of when you start and stop this “make up distance” in the log file.

hits.title: Guidelines for Tallest Hat Record Attempt | PDF - Scribd
hits.summary:
Tallest hat 28 March 2021b) The item must be made to commercial or professional standard.c) The item must be measured by a qualified surveyor in the presence of two independentwitnesses.
## Please make sure you supply the following evidence:
• One cover letter explaining the context of the record attempt.
Please indicate when and howthe item was made and measured, where it was made and/or is currently (or will be) displayed.Please also indicate your chosen witnesses and your record measurement.
Also please providefull details of the person(s)/organisation who has made the item and include a brief description ofthe production and material used.
• Two independent witness statements confirming that the rules above have been adhered toand must explicitly state the exact dimensions of the large item together with information of whenand where the item was made and by whom.
The statements must also describe the measuringprocess.• One surveyor’s report must be provided detailing the exact measurement taken as well asother measurements taken confirming that all rules were adhered to and providing evidence ofthe surveyor’s qualification.
• Photographic evidence is compulsory evidence for all record attempts.
Please providephotographs showing evidence of the measuring taking place as well as the construction of theitem.• Video evidence of the entire record attempt and the measurement process to confirm allmeasurements and that the guidelines have been adhered to and verify the details provided bythe independent witnesses.
The video evidence must also clearly show the measurement valuesdisplayed on the measurement device(s) used in the record attempt.• Media articles are not a compulsory evidence requirement.
If you have media coverage(newspaper, online, TV or radio) GWR please submit them as part of the evidence requirements.
...
Evidence checklist • Proof of commercial availability • Pre-approval of the design • Video evidence • Proof of qualifications • Cover letter • Witness statement 1 • Witness statement 2 • Surveyor's report • Photographic evidence • Video of measuring processes • Media articles
## Guinness World Records

hits.title: How are records measured? | Guinness World Records
hits.summary:
If your record idea is approved, we’ll send you the guidelines for your attempt.
Your guidelines are unique to your record and explain:
- **What the record is for and who can attempt it**
- ** The rules you must follow**
- ** How we measure the record**
- ** What evidence you need to provide**
You’ll find your guidelines on your account page as a link under your record title.
You can also download them as a PDF and share them with everyone involved in your attempt – participants, organisers and independent witnesses.
It’s vital that everyone reads and understands the rules/guidelines before the attempt.
...
**That’s why you must make an enquiry and wait for your official guidelines before you make your attempt.**
Most records are verified by uploading evidence for our Records Management Team to review.
For some attempts, you can have an official Guinness World Records Adjudicator at your event.

hits.title: Evidence help for a marathon world record attempt - Reddit
hits.summary:
'Fastest race-marathon' records Please make sure ALL these rules are followed: a)   Half and full marathon attempts must be done at an officially sanctioned race, certified by a national athletic governing body e.g.
USATF (USA Track and Field) or UKA (United Kingdom Athletics).
Ultramarathon races must be certified by the International Association of Athletics Federations (IAAF), the International Association of Ultrarunners (IAU) or a national athletic governing body.
For the purpose of this record races cannot be one-off races or be organised for the sole purpose of the record attempt.
Races that do not meet these requirements must be sent to Guinness World Records for pre-approval and whether they are accepted will be at our discretion.
b)   The attempt must be made on a pre-measured course as part of an organised race.
c)   Shorter race distances can take place on an athletics track.
Where the attempt is not done on an athletics track, the course must be measured and marked out by someone suitably qualified, such as a professional surveyor, who is also responsible for confirming the gradient over the full length of the course.
d)    A loud start signal recognized by all participants must be used.
e)    Timing must be done using a chronograph activated by photo-electric timing cells positioned at either end of the track.
f)    The runner must have a race number.
...
j)    The event must be overseen by two independent witnesses, one of whom must be the race director.
In the case that the person attempting the record is the race director, a further independent witness must oversee the attempt.
Please make sure you supply the following evidence: •    One cover letter explaining the context of the record attempt.
…
•    A statement from the race organiser/director must be provided for each race, confirming the full details of the race, that is was professional surveyed, that the individual ran and completed the race, their race number and finishing time.
•    Two independent witness statements from each race must be provided confirming that the rules above have been adhered to, must explicitly state the time taken to complete the race and verify that they have observed the participant at the start, finish and at other points during the race.
You can use the templates in the Guide to Your Evidence or prepare different Witness Statements as long as they follow GWR directives.
The race organiser/director statement will suffice as one of these two independent witness statements.
If the applicant is the race organiser/director, a second independent statement must be submitted.
•    Proof that the race is officially certified, sanctioned or recognized by the aforementioned international or national federations.
This can be included in the race organiser/director statement or it can be a printout from the official race website.
•    Photographic evidence is compulsory evidence for all record attempts.
Please provide photographs showing comprehensive evidence of the person attempting the record before, during and after the attempt.
High quality pictures will be considered for publication online and in the Guinness World Records book or related products.
•    Video evidence should be provided for this attempt where possible.
•    Media articles (newspaper, online, TV or radio) can be submitted as part of the evidence requirements.
This is not compulsory evidence.
...
Their guidelines state a full length video for the whole thing or a photo every mile, which is still quite tricky to organise!
